item.title: Picture?
item.content:encoded:
Will you ever add a picture to scp-3025?
If you cannot find one i would be glad to photoshop you a one!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cool imagery never really coheres into a satisfying story. With that many expedition logs, I want something to be learned or revealed by the end. The monsters, too, felt like they were added on just to ramp up the danger/weirdness, but didn't seem to tie in to any underlying theme. Besides the fact that it's only in the last section of the arricle that it's revealed that a dangerous, possibly uncontainable anomaly has been released by this thing, almost as a throw away line? B-3 seems like, in universe, it would earn its own scp entry, or at minimum a mention in the containment procedures of this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked some of the exploration logs. They’re the article’s greatest strength.

The descriptions outside the logs felt cumbersome and loaded with unnecessary detail that lacked pay-off.

That ties into my biggest problem, and maybe it’s just that I haven’t read the other articles or it could have gone over my head, but it doesn’t seem to have a tight narrative. I can’t figure out what the message is.

-1

item.title: Censoring random data
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-3025-B2 has a top speed of ███ kilometers per hour"
Why the hell would THAT info be blacked out?? Blacking out stuff for the sake of having blacked-out stuff is stupid. It's supposed to represent potentially dangerous information that the reader's imagination would go wild trying to figure out. My imagination doesn't care how many km/h it moves with, it's not interesting. It adds nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed watching this SCP evolving from 'thing what sets people on fire' to 'thing what sets people on fire because they've been transported to an alternate universe/reality/plane of existence etc. which has extreme temperatures and its own lifeforms'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked a lot of this, but in the end it doesn't feel like it comes together enough for me to upvote. Much to the tone of what psul said - there are monsters and a hellscape, but nothing that really tell me why it exists or any kind of twist that justifies its existence. When you introduced the concept of large flying objects, I was waiting for that to be the payoff (I was thinking cloaked jets or something) but I never really got it. I looked at the crosslinked skips to see if perhaps there was context there that I was missing, but they seem to be only loosely linked and don't really add anything to one another redacted because I hadn't gotten to the end of 3066 when I typed this, and now that I have, I actually see that you did manage to tie these together in the end.

On that note, I'm going to change my review to say: I think that the best way to improve this article would be to make the link to 3066 more obvious. To me, at least, having the origin story was necessary to being able to fully enjoy the narrative, and I think similar people might get the same lackluster impression that I first did without it. In general, this whole series could be crosslinked better, which I think would help the ratings for all four.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A feather which causes things in a very particular area to burn (but some things survive the burning) which creates a portal to the Plane of Fire, and sometimes things come back from the Plane of Fire. It all feels a bit too disjointed and confusing. I think there's potential here, because I like plane exploration, but I think the other effects rather get in the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this.one minor concern, if i
understood this correctly. And it could theoretically consume/burn the entire earth.unless it's anomalous properties where discoverd in a sealed area (which is unlikely) wouldn't it burn the entire earth?if you count earths atmosphere as a enclosed space

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really bad things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what happens if you drop it in the ocean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is what I get for using shortcuts…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon reappearance, the devices sustain minor damage, but are otherwise intact. An instance of SCP-3025-A is discovered following this test.

6 minutes after recovery, an instance of SCP-3025-A is discovered inside the test chamber.

Did you mean SCP-3025-B in these cases?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Check out the rest of the Linville Arc!

SCP-3076: Avidity

SCP-3058: Concavity

SCP-3066: Absurdity

item.title: Errors
item.content:encoded:
"It is hypothesized that things affected by SCP-3025 enter continue to exist in an extradimentional space (SCP-3025-A) "
Haven't they seen it and got samples from it?
"Pleas"
Minor spelling error in the logs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disjointed and lacking a coherent plot or theme. I was hoping that the various explorations would come together to reveal where everyone was, but instead they just sit there, people die, and then Monsters! for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You might be thinking of Egyptian mythology, where the feather is weighed against people's souls to judge their purity. In answer to your question, no, that wasn't what I was thinking while writing this SCP, but you can interpret it however you like!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this related to any mythology of sorts? It kinda feels like it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't worry, it's not hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Radar trackers and multi-spectral cameras are supplied. Objective is to determine the nature of the being encountered during Expedition Gamma.

I'd refrain from using the word 'being', especially at this point. They have no idea about that yet.

Command: It’ll be okay, ████. Just try to find shelter. There should be something in the mountains

Is command using her name? Bit unprofessional, but understandable I guess.

Are they sending in D-Class in a tank? Even if they don't want to lose MTF personnel, I can't imagine the Foundation trusting D-Class with this. What if they return and decide that they've had enough of their imprisonment? They'd be in a freaking tank. It's going to be hard to explain to your superiors why the Site suddenly has a gigantic hole in it and a tank is missing.

The rest of it is really interesting though. I just hope it's not Hell. That would be cliché. And on the nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one would be a lot stronger if you dialed it down some. "Every time we send activate it it gets worse. And stuff manifests. Last time a huge mechanical feather dipping spider came out and now runs around the neighborhood uncontained. Shall we activate it again to send in a team of criminals in a tank?" "Yes, science demands it."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I went ahead and made a few edits. I better defined the boundaries of the SCP's affect and made sure to mention that they knew items were coming back a day after they combustion, which is why they decided to bring in the GPS and camera. As for the tank, I don't know. It seems reasonable enough that there would be a few ex-military D-class, so it's possible. To be honest, I wanted to write about the extreme case, where the Foundation just goes all out. If its too much, I'm not stubborn about keeping it.*

*Based on the comments I've gotten so far, I went ahead and got rid of Expedition Epsilon. It didn't really add much to the story and was stretching suspension of disbelief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote as well, for precisely these reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, is the wall of the room qualifies as "object"? If not how about stuff attached/part of the wall but not necessarily function as the wall i.e. light switch or embedded nails? what about the container of the liquid? stuff submerged within same liquid? Is opening a hole, i.e. to drop objects within the chamber, qualifies as expanding the enclosed space? what if something is connected via cable i.e. CCTV cameras? is the cable qualifies as object along with the camera?

The vague definition of what qualifies as "Objects" and "Enclosed space" break the article a bit for me… although it's pretty good concept IMO. no vote for now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite enjoyed the concept of this but there are a lot of issues with it that ruined it for me. A bunch of small grammar, spelling and format issues. The test where they add a camera and GPS tracker to something they think is a spontaneous combustion effect - why would they do that? And sending a tank with a bunch of D-Class? Come on.
Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: a flight feather is known as a primary feather unless you're referring to the broader, shorter secondaries.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I came up with this while listening to a Logic album.

Thanks to Ehksidian and Roget for the feedback!
